zh.Presteigne, Llythyfnwg, Radnorshire, Wal: 英國威爾斯拉德諾斯爾普雷斯泰尼
zh.lifeSummary: 當Hywel "Howell the Less"  Vychan ap Llewellyn於大約1210年出生在英國威爾斯拉德諾斯爾普雷斯泰尼時，他的父親Llewelyn ap Mredydd Vychan38歲，母親Mabel Ap Ifor Bach Fitz Robert27歲。他和Sara le Sore至少有1個兒子。他去世於1243年享年34歲。

zh-hans.Presteigne, Llythyfnwg, Radnorshire, Wal: 英国威尔斯拉德諾斯爾普雷斯泰尼
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hywel "Howell the Less"  Vychan ap Llewellyn于大约1210年出生在英国威尔斯拉德諾斯爾普雷斯泰尼时，他的父亲Llewelyn ap Mredydd Vychan38岁，母亲Mabel Ap Ifor Bach Fitz Robert27岁。他和Sara le Sore至少有1个儿子。他去世于1243年享年34岁。

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Abt  .

Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): habitational name from Apt, the Yiddish name of Opatów in Świętokrzyskie Voivodeship, Poland. The placename (in German, Yiddish, and Polish) is from a root meaning ‘abbot’, the place having been named for the local abbey. Compare   Abt  .

variants.name: Abt
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Horst, Johann, Kurt.

German and Dutch: from Middle Low German or Middle Dutch abt ‘abbot’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a servant of an abbot or a nickname for someone thought to resemble an abbot in some way. In some instances it may have been a topographic or habitational name referring to a house named with this word.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): variant of   Abt  , a habitational name from Opatów in Poland.

variants.name: Apter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Avrohom, Bronya, Eliezer, Naftali.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Apt (see   Apt  ).
English: habitational name from Aptor in Marldon, Devon, composed of Old English uppe ‘up, above’ + torr ‘rocky outcrop’.

nameMeanings.name: Llewellyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: from the personal name Llywelyn, derived from British Celtic Lugubelinos, itself from the divine name Lugus + an uncertain second element often used alone as a name. Through association with llew ‘lion’, it was altered to Llewelyn. This was the name of two princes of north Wales who held the Normans at bay for many years in the 13th century. Etymologically speaking, the form with a fourth -l- is an error, not found as an original Welsh name, but this is now much the most common spelling in the US.

variants.name: Lewelling
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Llewellyn  .

variants.name: Lewellen
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Llewellyn  .

variants.name: Lewallen
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Llewellyn  .

variants.name: Louallen
meanings: Altered form of Welsh Llewelyn (see Llewellyn ).

variants.name: Luellen
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Llewellyn  .

variants.name: Welling
meanings: English: habitational name from Welwyn (Hertfordshire) or Welling in Bexley (Kent), with a suspicion of an alternative unidentified source. The Hertfordshire placename means ‘at the willow trees’, from Old English wel(i)gum, dative plural form of welig ‘willow’. The Kent placename may derive from Old English wella ‘well, spring, stream’ + the placename forming suffix -ing. In some cases this name may represent a development of Llewellyn , particularly near the Welsh border.
German: habitational name from any of various places in the Rhineland called Welling or Wellingen.

variants.name: Luallen
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Llewellyn  .

variants.name: Lewellyn
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Llewellyn  .

variants.name: Llewelyn
meanings: Welsh: variant of   Llewellyn  .

nameMeanings.name: Hywel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional: originally a byname from a vocabulary word meaning ‘eminent, conspicuous’; it was common in the Middle Ages and was revived in the 20th-century.

variants.name: Hywell
meanings: Traditional: originally a byname from a vocabulary word meaning ‘eminent, conspicuous’; it was common in the Middle Ages and was revived in the 20th-century.

variants.name: Howell
meanings: Anglicized form of the Welsh name Hywel , or a transferred use of the surname derived from that name.

nameMeanings.name: "Howell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of the Welsh name Hywel , or a transferred use of the surname derived from that name.

variants.name: Hywel
meanings: Traditional: originally a byname from a vocabulary word meaning ‘eminent, conspicuous’; it was common in the Middle Ages and was revived in the 20th-century.

nameMeanings.name: the
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Either a variant spelling of Ty or a transferred use of the surname, a local name for someone who lived by a common pasture, Middle English tye.

nameMeanings.name: Less"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usually a girl's name, a short form of   Jessie   or   Jessica .  As a boy's name, it is a short form of   Jesse   or   Jessie .  It is also used independently, both for boys and girls.

variants.name: Jesse
meanings: Meaning ‘gift’ in Hebrew; it is borne by the father of King David (1 Samuel 16), from whose line (according to the New Testament) Jesus was ultimately descended. It was popular among the Puritans, and is still used frequently in the United States, less so in Britain. As a girl's name it is a respelling of   Jessie .  Notable American bearers have included the outlaw  Jesse James  (  1847–82 ), the athlete  Jesse Owens  (  1913–80 ), and the politician  Jesse Jackson  (  b. 1941 ).

variants.name: Jessica
meanings: Apparently of Shakespearean origin. This was the name of the daughter of Shylock in The Merchant of Venice ( 1596 ). Shakespeare's source has not been established, but he presumably intended it to pass as a typically Jewish name. It may be from a biblical name that appeared, in the translations available in Shakespeare's day, as Jesca (Genesis 11:29; Iscah in the Authorized Version). This occurs in a somewhat obscure genealogical passage; Iscah appears to have been Abraham's niece. Notable bearers of the name include the British actress Jessica Tandy ( 1909–94 ), the British writer Jessica Mitford ( 1917–96 ), and the American actress Jessica Lange ( b. 1949 ). The name has been extremely popular since the 1990s.

nameMeanings.name: Vychan
meanings: Welsh: A Welsh name of great antiquity, from vyckan, little, small; i.e. small in stature. Compare the surname Bychan and the Armoric Le Bihan.

en.lifeSummary: When Hywel "Howell the Less"  Vychan ap Llewellyn was born about 1210, in Presteigne, Radnorshire, Wales, United Kingdom, his father, Llewelyn ap Mredydd Vychan, was 38 and his mother, Mabel Ap Ifor Bach Fitz Robert, was 27. He had at least 1 son with Sara le Sore. He died in 1243, at the age of 34.

fr.Presteigne, Llythyfnwg, Radnorshire, Wal: Presteigne, Radnorshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hywel "Howell the Less"  Vychan ap Llewellyn est né environ 1210, à Presteigne, Radnorshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Llewelyn ap Mredydd Vychan, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Mabel Ap Ifor Bach Fitz Robert, avait 27 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Sara le Sore. Il est décédé en 1243, à l’âge de 34 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hywel "Howell the Less"  Vychan ap Llewellyn um 1210 in Presteigne, Radnorshire, Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Llewelyn ap Mredydd Vychan 38 und seine Mutter Mabel Ap Ifor Bach Fitz Robert 27. Er hatte mit Sara le Sore mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1243 im Alter von 34 Jahren.
